findings.title: Unconditional closesocket call with possible invalid socket
findings.rationale: The change removed the check for socket_ != INVALID_SOCKET before calling closesocket. Windows API requires a valid socket handle for closesocket; passing INVALID_SOCKET (typically -1) causes undefined behavior (e.g., crash or error). The old code safely skipped the call when socket was already closed.
findings.suggestion: Reintroduce the validity check before calling closesocket

evidence.ref: Windows API documentation: closesocket requires valid socket handle
spdlog coding guidelines: handle socket state transitions safely
